title: Understand: The Close of the Convention
description: Check your understanding the close of the Constitutional Convention in this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: The Road to Reform, the Constitutional Convention, and the Battle for Ratification
The Close of the Convention: Benjamin Franklin's Closing Speech and the Dissenters
Understand: The Close of the Convention

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the sentence.**

At the Constitutional Convention, Benjamin Franklin disapproved of several parts of the Constitution,  \_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: but he encouraged his fellow delegates to sign the Constitution anyway
and he refused to sign the Constitution until those parts were removed
and he insisted that he would never approve of the Constitution
so he wrote essays opposing the ratification of the Constitution

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these famous founders refused to sign the Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: George Mason
Alexander Hamilton
James Madison
George Washington

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did three of the delegates refuse to sign the Constitution on September 17, 1787?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They had already left Philadelphia for their home states.
They thought it was too long.
It didn’t include a bill of rights.
They wanted to establish a monarchy in the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was Benjamin Franklin’s reply when asked what type of government the delegates had created?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: "A monarchy, more or less."
"A direct democracy."
"A republic, if you can keep it."
"A government that will never work."

assessment_items.question_content:
**What do we now call the three delegates who refused to sign the Constitution on September 17, 1787—the final day of the Constitutional Convention? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Dissenters
The Troublemakers
The Rebels
The Loyalists

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was true about the delegates to the Constitutional Convention?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They crafted a stronger central government than the one that came before it.
They created a weaker central government than the one that came before it.
They viewed political parties as essential to making the Constitution work.
They wrote a Constitution that included a bill of rights.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was one key reason George Mason refused to sign the Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The framers refused to include a bill of rights in the Constitution.
The Constitution didn’t end the institution of slavery.
The legislature did not have representation based on population.
He wanted a stronger federal government, particularly a stronger executive.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true about Elbridge Gerry?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He refused to sign the Constitution.
After hearing Benjamin Franklin speak, he reluctantly signed the Constitution.
He wrote essays to convince states to ratify the Constitution.
He did not attend the Constitutional Convention.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these people opposed the Constitution during the battle over its ratification at the Constitutional Convention?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Elbridge Gerry
Mercy Otis Warren
Samuel Adams
Benjamin Franklin
Thomas Jefferson

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which clause of the Constitution did George Mason think unreasonably expanded the power of the national government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Necessary and Proper Clause
The Full Faith and Credit Clause
The Privileges and Immunity Clause
The First Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the main purpose of Benjamin Franklin’s closing speech at the Constitutional Convention?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To list his objections to the new Constitution
To encourage the other delegates to sign the Constitution
To argue for the addition of a Bill of Rights to the Constitution
To persuade the delegates to appoint George Washington as president

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the Dissenters' fears about the new Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It gave too much power to state governments, weakening the national government’s ability to maintain order.
It was created by delegates from only half of the states, so it was unlikely to be ratified by the other states.
It violated the democratic principles of equality for all by allowing slavery to continue in the United States.
It increased the powers of the central government, which could lead to the violation of American’s rights.
